stoplight.name: Stoplight
stoplight.short_description: Stoplight is a design-first platform for building, documenting, mocking, and governing OpenAPI-based APIs, now operating under SmartBear.

features.description: A visual editor with comprehensive OpenAPI and JSON Schema support lets teams design and prototype APIs without writing complex syntax by hand.
features.title: Visual API Editor

features.description: Mock servers are generated automatically, stay up-to-date with the API design, and support request and response validation for testing.
features.title: Automatic Mock Servers

features.description: API documentation is generated automatically alongside design work, with a live preview available directly in the editor.
features.title: Interactive Documentation

features.description: Built-in and custom style guides enforce design consistency, with shared style guides available on Pro and Enterprise plans to promote governance across an API portfolio.
features.title: Style Guides & Governance

features.description: Schemas and components can be reused across multiple APIs, with centralized updates that propagate to all dependent APIs.
features.title: Component Reusability

features.description: Integrates with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure so teams can work with API design artifacts directly in their existing Git workflow.
features.title: Git Sync

features.description: The Pro Team plan adds LDAP/SAML SSO, workspace groups, and activity logs for larger organizations.
features.title: SSO & Activity Logs

features.description: Stoplight maintains three open-source projects: Spectral (linting), Prism (mocking), and Elements (documentation).
features.title: Open Source Tooling

pros: Free perpetual plan available for a single user and project
All paid plans include a 14-day free trial
Visual editor lowers the barrier to working with OpenAPI and JSON Schema
Built-in and shared style guides support API governance across teams
Reusable components reduce duplication across an API portfolio
Open-source tools (Spectral, Prism, Elements) are available alongside the hosted platform
cons: Paid plans charge per additional user beyond the included seats, which can increase cost for larger teams
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published on the site
Free plan is limited to 1 user and 1 project
Now operates as part of SmartBear following its 2023 acquisition, rather than as a fully independent company

faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight offers a Free plan for $0 that includes 1 user, 1 project, the API editor, visual designer, interactive docs, and instant/local mock servers.
faqs.question: Is there a free plan for Stoplight?

faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans (Basic, Startup, Pro Team) include a 14-day free trial.
faqs.question: Does Stoplight offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight's website and pricing remain active under the Stoplight brand, though the company now operates as part of SmartBear Software following an acquisition announced in 2023.
faqs.question: Is Stoplight still an active, standalone product?

faqs.answer: Stoplight integrates with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure for Git-based API design workflows.
faqs.question: What Git providers does Stoplight integrate with?

faqs.answer: Yes, Stoplight maintains three open-source projects: Spectral for linting, Prism for mocking, and Elements for documentation.
faqs.question: Does Stoplight provide open-source tools?

faqs.answer: It creates dynamic, automatically updating org charts showing reporting lines, departments, and cross-functional teams, built from HRIS data or an Excel upload.
faqs.question: What does Pingboard's org chart tool do?

faqs.answer: Yes, Pingboard includes an employee directory with custom profiles showing roles, departments, and team composition.
faqs.question: Does Pingboard have an employee directory?

psono.name: Psono
psono.short_description: Self-hosted, open-source password manager for teams with client-side encryption, free for up to 10 users.

features.description: Credentials are encrypted client-side before transport and storage, using multiple layers of encryption.
features.title: Client-side encryption

features.description: Deploy Psono on your own servers for full control over where credential data is stored.
features.title: Self-hosted deployment

features.description: Psono is also available as a hosted SaaS edition for teams that don't want to manage their own infrastructure.
features.title: SaaS option

features.description: The source code is publicly available, which the company says allows for independent security audits.
features.title: Open-source codebase

features.description: Teams can securely share passwords and credentials with other members through encrypted sharing.
features.title: Encrypted credential sharing

features.description: Apps are available for macOS, Windows, and Linux, plus mobile apps for iOS and Android, and browser extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge.
features.title: Cross-platform apps

pros: Open-source code allows for independent security audits
Free plan includes all business features for up to 10 users
Self-hosted option gives full control over data residency
Available across desktop, mobile, and browser platforms
Rated 4.7/5 on G2 and 4.5/5 on the Chrome Web Store
cons: Pricing for teams larger than 10 users is not published and requires contacting the company
Self-hosting requires server management expertise
No published details on enterprise SLA pricing

faqs.answer: Yes. Psono offers all business features free for up to 10 users.
faqs.question: Is Psono free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes. Psono can be deployed on-premise on your own servers, or used as a hosted SaaS edition.
faqs.question: Can Psono be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Yes. Psono's source code is publicly available, which the company says allows for independent security audits.
faqs.question: Is Psono open source?

faqs.answer: Psono has apps for macOS, Windows, and Linux, mobile apps for iOS and Android, and extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge.
faqs.question: What platforms does Psono support?

features: Professional support and SLAs available on request
